content.boundary: Applies system-wide as the counterfactual baseline against which S1-S4 are evaluated; involves no new policy instrument, only continuation of existing law and demographic trend
content.change_lever: No legislative or administrative change to admissions, capacity, or selection age; the status-quo legal and governance framework (Act CXC, free choice, centralized deferred-acceptance algorithm) remains fully intact, and consolidation occurs only as a byproduct of enforcing existing statutory minimum class-size rules (26/28/34) against falling local cohorts
content.name: Demographically-driven passive contraction (no-new-policy baseline)
content.system_problem: Hungary's birth cohorts are declining steeply and structurally (77,500 births in 2024, ~72,000 projected 2025, TFR at 1.31, embedded for 20+ years), and roughly 40% of primary schools already have zero students attempting selective-track entrance exams (60% in villages), meaning demographic mechanics alone are already reshaping the selective track's footprint without policy action
